payload.title: Multi-writer pull-cursor skip: hosted pull must order by a server-assigned sequence
payload.description: Found by the SCP-224 realtime e2e (concurrent two-writer convergence). pg/store.js pullEvents orders by event_id (ULID) and the client cursor is a ULID high-water mark. Under concurrent writers an event created earlier (lower ULID) can be pushed to the hub LATER - after a puller's cursor already passed that ULID - so pull-since-cursor skips it permanently and the replicas never converge. Pre-existing in the sync-cursor protocol (ADR 0002); invisible to single-writer manual sync, surfaced by realtime multi-writer. FIX: give events a server-assigned monotonic seq (bigserial) and make pullEvents order+filter by seq (cursor = max seq, opaque to the client) so an event inserted later always has a higher cursor regardless of its ULID. Disk/ULID union is unchanged; only the hosted pull ORDERING changes.
